content:
### 最新の関連X投稿 (2026-04-07 時点、Latestモードで取得)

1. **本文**: India’s ₹1 lakh crore Semicon Mission 2.0 prioritises HBM memory packaging amid AI demand surge. Global HBM share: SK Hynix 57%, Samsung 22%, Micron 21%. Micron’s $2.5B Gujarat plant targets tens of millions chips in 2026, scaling to 100M+ in 2027.  
   **URL**: https://x.com/AvinashKS14/status/2041442650533065086

2. **本文**: SAMSUNG, SK HYNIX and MICRON - HBM - high bandwidth memory is must for AI-Data Center  
   **URL**: https://x.com/UmeshHi57833329/status/2041422924712968227

3. **本文**: India pushes ahead with Semicon Mission 2.0 ₹1 lakh crore focus now on HBM memory packaging driven by rising AI demand Global HBM landscape SK Hynix 57 percent Samsung 22 percent Micron 21 percent Micron’s 2.5 billion dollar Gujarat plant aims to produce tens of millions of chips in 2026 Scaling beyond 100 million units by 2027 A major step in India’s semiconductor ambitions  
   **URL**: https://x.com/wise_scholor/status/2041362115252863472

4. **本文**: There are only 3 companies in the world making High Bandwidth Memory (HBM) chips. SK Hynix(South Korea) Samsung(South Korea) Micron(US) 80 pct of these chips are just from South Korea. Below is the south korea Index in last 1 year led by these chip makers.  
   **URL**: https://x.com/tushar9590/status/2041194665056374937

5. **本文**: $MU $DRAM $EWY Samsung and SK Hynix have already locked in nearly two years’ worth of AI memory supply, most of their HBM and high-end server DDR5 capacity is reserved through long-term contracts with Big Tech. Microsoft and Google are actively pushing for long-term supply agreements (LTAs). SK Hynix is in final negotiations with Microsoft for a 3-year server DDR5 contract rumored to be worth tens of trillions of KRW (~tens of billions of USD), including minimum price guarantees and potential advance payments. Similar discussions with Google cover HBM and general server DRAM. DRAM spot prices are expected to surge 58-63% quarter-over-quarter in Q2 2026 (per TrendForce’s March 2026 report), fueled by North American Big Tech cloud investments and long-term contract negotiations. Memory prices have already more than doubled from early 2025 levels by late 2025, with DRAM inventory levels dropping to critically low 2 - 4 weeks. This trend is expected to stabilize profitability and fab utilization for Samsung and SK Hynix while reducing procurement uncertainty for hyperscalers. New capacity expansions are underway, but meaningful relief for general memory is not expected until 2027–2028.  
   **URL**: https://x.com/TradexWhisperer/status/2041191000450990574

6. **本文**: AI: $MU, $005930.KS, and SK Hynix production schedules key to easing HBM shortage, analyst says, with potential impact on consumer electronics prices. https://spectrum.ieee.org/high-bandwidth-memory-shortage  
   **URL**: https://x.com/rrsrch/status/2041184354232111146

7. **本文**: $MU down 20% from its high in three weeks. SK Hynix ADR could raise $10 billion and give investors another DRAM option. Here's what that actually means: more capital flowing into the HBM memory space, not away from it. A SK Hynix listing doesn't shrink Micron's order book. Every AI data center still needs HBM3E. Demand isn't splitting — it's growing faster than both companies can supply it. The near-term pressure is real. The thesis isn't broken. $MU at a 20% discount to its recent high, with HBM supply sold out through 2026, is a different conversation than the chart suggests. $MU $NVDA  
   **URL**: https://x.com/roboinvestAI/status/2041136584410509468
